mission.title: Install and Configure Docker Desktop Kubernetes
mission.description: Docker Desktop for Mac provides a seamless local development environment for building and testing containerized applications using Kubernetes. It simplifies the setup process by integrating Docker and Kubernetes into a single application. Key trade-offs include limited scalability compared to cloud solutions and potential performance overhead on local machines.

steps.title: Download Docker Desktop for Mac
steps.description:
This step installs Docker Desktop, which includes Kubernetes support.

```bash

open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac

```

> This opens the download page for Docker Desktop.

steps.title: Install Docker Desktop
steps.description:
Run the downloaded installer to set up Docker Desktop on your Mac.

```bash

hdiutil attach ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

cp -r /Volumes/Docker/Docker.app /Applications/

hdiutil detach /Volumes/Docker

```

> Mount the downloaded Docker image.
> Copy Docker.app to the Applications folder.
> Unmount the Docker image.

steps.title: Start Docker Desktop and Enable Kubernetes
steps.description:
Launch Docker Desktop and enable Kubernetes in the settings.

```bash

open /Applications/Docker.app

sleep 30 && open -a 'System Preferences' 'Docker'

osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click button "Kubernetes" of window "Preferences"'

osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click checkbox "Enable Kubernetes" of window "Preferences"'

osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click button "Apply & Restart" of window "Preferences"'

```

> This starts Docker Desktop.
> Wait for Docker to start and open settings.
> Navigate to the Kubernetes settings.
> Enable Kubernetes.
> Apply changes and restart Docker.
commands: osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click button "Kubernetes" of window "Preferences"'
osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click checkbox "Enable Kubernetes" of window "Preferences"'
osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click button "Apply & Restart" of window "Preferences"'

steps.title: Verify Kubernetes Installation
steps.description:
Check if Kubernetes is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl version --short

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command shows the client and server versions of Kubernetes.
> This command lists the nodes in the Kubernetes cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Docker Desktop
uninstall.description:
This step removes Docker Desktop and all associated Kubernetes resources. Data loss may occur.

```bash

osascript -e 'tell application "Docker" to quit'

rm -rf /Applications/Docker.app

rm -rf ~/Library/Containers/com.docker.docker

rm -rf ~/.kube

```

> Quit Docker Desktop.
> Remove Docker Desktop application.
> Remove Docker's application data.
> Remove Kubernetes configuration and data.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Docker Desktop
upgrade.description:
Ensure you have a backup of your Docker images and containers before upgrading.

```bash

docker save -o ~/docker_images_backup.tar $(docker images -q)

open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac

hdiutil attach ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

cp -r /Volumes/Docker/Docker.app /Applications/

hdiutil detach /Volumes/Docker

open /Applications/Docker.app

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup all Docker images.
> Download the latest version of Docker Desktop.
> Mount the downloaded Docker image.
> Copy the new Docker.app to the Applications folder.
> Unmount the Docker image.
> Start Docker Desktop after the upgrade.
> Verify that Kubernetes is still running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Docker Desktop may not have started Kubernetes properly.

**Fix:**
Restart Docker Desktop and ensure Kubernetes is enabled in settings.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl: command not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Ensure Docker Desktop is installed and running, which includes kubectl.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:6443: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubernetes API server is not running.

**Fix:**
Check Docker Desktop settings to ensure Kubernetes is enabled and running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubernetes may be taking too long to start.

**Fix:**
Wait for a few minutes and check the status again with 'kubectl get nodes'.

versionNotes.changes: Improved Kubernetes integration and updated to Kubernetes v1.30.

resolution.summary:
You should see output similar to:

NAME                 STATUS   ROLES    AGE   VERSION
docker-desktop      Ready    master   10m   v1.30.0
codeSnippets: osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click button "Kubernetes" of window "Preferences"'
osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click checkbox "Enable Kubernetes" of window "Preferences"'

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Mac meets the minimum requirements for Docker Desktop.
